behaviors.description: `=SUM(A1:A10)` returns the correct total for A1..A10.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is function semantics (range expansion + SUM aggregation) within evaluator/function logic. MOCK-MISS: no required module handoff beyond local expression evaluation. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text does not require cross-module state propagation; rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because no OS/network/subprocess or AI steps.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory sheet with numeric values in A1..A10, evaluate `=SUM(A1:A10)`, assert exact numeric result and basic type handling.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a formula cell (e.g., `=B1+1`, `B1:=A1*2`) yields correct cascaded value.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundaries: reference resolver + dependency/evaluation order + formula evaluator must cooperate. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) no LLM stage handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Build a workbook in memory with `A1=3`, `B1:=A1*2`, `C1:=B1+1`; run normal engine evaluation path and assert `B1=6`, `C1=7` with correct dependency tracking.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1->B1->A1) is detected and surfaced as circular-reference error.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is interaction between reference graph construction, cycle detection, and evaluator error propagation (not just isolated cycle algorithm). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Set `A1:=B1+1` and `B1:=A1+1` via standard workbook APIs; trigger recalculation and assert deterministic circular-reference error (and no hang/loop).

behaviors.description: `VLOOKUP` exact match returns correct value; missing key returns error.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is built-in function correctness (lookup semantics + not-found branch). MOCK-MISS: no required inter-module failure boundary beyond local function evaluation context. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text does not require dependency propagation/orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate `VLOOKUP` formulas against an in-memory table range for found and not-found keys; assert returned value and specific lookup error.

behaviors.description: Nested `IF` conditions produce correct branch outputs.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is expression evaluation semantics (comparison + nested branch execution) inside evaluator/function logic. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text targets function logic, not cross-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Parametrized in-memory evaluations for `A1` values (e.g., 5, 15, 25) against nested IF formula; assert `low/medium/high` outputs.

behaviors.description: `CONCATENATE` joins with separator and skips empty cells.
behaviors.justification: This is pure function behavior (string join + empty handling rules). Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because no cross-module interaction is necessary to expose this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate concatenation formulas over local cell values including empty cells; assert exact joined string and absence of extra separators/blanks.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (`=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves when target sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is sheet namespace resolution + reference binding + evaluator cooperation across workbook modules. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook with `Sheet2!A1=10`, formula on `Sheet1` referencing `Sheet2!A1+1`; run full in-memory recalc and assert result `11` and correct sheet binding.

behaviors.description: Updating a referenced cell triggers dependent recalculation in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is module interaction: dependency graph invalidation, scheduler/topological ordering, and evaluator execution. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no OS/network/subprocess requirement, no AI output dependency, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Set a dependency chain/diamond (e.g., A1->B1,C1->D1), evaluate once, mutate A1, trigger engine recalc, assert updated dependent values and observed recomputation order respects topology.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula text and prior dependent computed values.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears across undo stack/history, formula storage, and dependency recalculation state restoration. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependency, no multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Apply formula edit through normal API, verify changed dependents, perform undo, assert original formula text plus all downstream values match pre-edit snapshot.

behaviors.description: Save workbook to custom binary and load back with identical formulas/values and unchanged float precision.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is serializer/deserializer + workbook model round-trip integrity. Rejection-gate: considered System due 'to disk' wording, but behavior text focuses on data fidelity/format correctness, not OS-specific filesystem behavior (permissions, events, locking, network FS); this can be caught with local module wiring and controlled file/stream I/O. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no AI dependency and no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Construct workbook with representative formulas and sensitive floating-point values, save via production serializer, reload via production loader, assert formula text equality and value equality within strict precision policy.
